perturbation.original_text: "End User Agreement" means Supp l i e r ' s s t andard l i cense ag reement s , wh ich a re ava i l ab le a t https://walabot.com/walabot-home, which governs each End User's right to use the Product, as amended by Supplier in its sole discretion from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: "End User Agreement" means Supp l i e r ' s s t andard l i cense ag reement s , wh ich a re ava i l ab le a t https://walabot.com/walabot-home, which governs each End User's right to use the Product, and cannot be amended, changed or modified by Supplier, unless prior written consent from the reseller.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows the Supplier to amend the End User Agreement at its sole discretion. The modified text now states that the End User Agreement cannot be amended without the Reseller's consent. This directly contradicts the supplier's right to make ammendments alone without the Reseller's written consent, especially in section 3.1, which states that the reseller shall comply with all applicable laws and regulations, this creates an ambiguity as to whose End User agreement to comply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: By fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, the Reseller shall: (i) comply with all applicable laws and regulations; (ii) use materials and branding solely as determined by the Supplier (subject to the right of the Reseller to make changes to such materials as are described in Section 2.4); (iii) attend training and sales meetings as required by the Supplier, so as to be able to describe, demonstrate, and sell the Products adequately; (iv) make sure that all the End Users are signing the End User Agreement, and in the event that any End User does not comply with these terms, Reseller shall bear all liability associated with said End User; and (v) make no representations or warranties with respect to the Products other than those provided in the Products' documentation;
perturbation.justification: The modified text stating that the End User Agreement cannot be amended without the Reseller's consent directly contradicts the original text's provision allowing the Supplier to amend the agreement at its sole discretion. This conflict creates ambiguity and potential legal issues, as outlined in the Perturbation Explanation, especially considering the Reseller's obligations in the Contradicted Text (e.g., complying with laws and ensuring End User Agreement compliance). This ambiguity regarding the applicable End User Agreement constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Supplier shall be fully responsible for shipping the Products to the Reseller at the address of the Reseller specified in each Order (or as otherwise communicated by Reseller to Supplier under this Agreement) and will obtain and provide for itself and/or on behalf of Reseller, any and all registrations, licenses, permits, certificates and all other documentation and information required for the exportation, importation, storage, marketing and otherwise resale of the Products in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Supplier shall use reasonable efforts to ship the Products to the Reseller at the address of the Reseller specified in each Order (or as otherwise communicated by Reseller to Supplier under this Agreement) and will try to obtain any and all registrations, licenses, permits, certificates and all other documentation and information required for the exportation, importation, storage, marketing and otherwise resale of the Products in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Supplier 'shall be fully responsible' for shipping and obtaining documentation. The modified text changes this to 'shall use reasonable efforts' and 'will try to obtain', making the obligation less definitive. This change introduces an ambiguity regarding the Supplier's responsibility, directly contradicting any other sections of the agreement that depends on the Supplier being fully responsible for these tasks, for example, Section 4.4 which talks about traceability of the delivered products
perturbation.contradicted_text: Immediately upon delivery of the Products at the Reseller's facilities within the Territory ("Arrival Date"), Reseller shall visually inspect the Products and the appropriate documents, and employ good receiving/delivering procedures, verification of the Products to confirm origin, identity, quantity, labeled storage conditions, integrity of the packaging as well as any other inspection, as may be required by applicable laws or regulations. Reseller shall ensure that the Products are delivered with appropriate information and documentation, and shall not make any changes to the Products or to the packaging or labeling thereof, or to the legal documents included in the Products' packages, without Supplier's prior written consent (subject to the right of the Reseller to make changes to such materials as are described in Section 2.4). Reseller shall ensure traceability of the delivered Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposes a strict responsibility on the supplier for shipping and documentation, while the modified text softens this obligation by using phrases like 'reasonable efforts' and 'will try to obtain'. This creates ambiguity and directly contradicts the contradicted text, which relies on the supplier's full responsibility for delivering the Products with the appropriate documentation to ensure traceability. The change weakens the supplier's commitment, making it unclear whether they are fully accountable for documentation and delivery as required by the Reseller to 'ensure traceability of the delivered Products.'
